title: O Love divine, how sweet Thou art
firstLine: O Love divine, how sweet Thou art,
publicDomainBasis: Charles Wesley (1707–1788), Lowell Mason (1792–1872). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
O Love divine, how sweet Thou art,
When shall I find my willing heart
All taken up by Thee?
My thirsty spirit faints to prove
The greatness of redeeming love,
The love of Christ to me.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Stronger His love than death and hell,
Its riches are unsearchable:
The first-born sons of light
Desire in vain its depths to see;
They cannot reach the mystery,
The length, and breadth, and height.

sections.label: Verse 3
Verse 4
